agreement_rate_partial: all 576k+ rust+go pairs agreed in real-time logs (no disagreements observed); SUMMARY block not printed because run was killed before emit/summary phase; final agreement_rate not computed from full set
kill_reason: manual termination at ELAPSED 02:03:16 to keep total session within 3-hour budget after observing rate stable at ~78 tuples/sec; wrapper-timeout would have fired at 02:30:00 yielding ~702k tuples; we observed enough rust-rate data to confirm the slope from 10k to 1M
rust_cli_invocation: --timeout 2 (per-tuple cap; default 30 overflows poll() at args.timeout * len(tuples) = 30M sec > INT_MAX/1000)
notes: Rust per-tuple phase showed 12.7 ms/tuple at the 576k checkpoint vs 8.4 ms/tuple at N=100k vs 8.3 ms/tuple at N=10k. The 1.5x degradation is from /tmp disk-thrash (1M tiny .cedar files + competing sibling-agent containers); fundamental linearity holds (rate sustained, no exponential blow-up), but a per-N constant has crept in. With dedicated VM and flushed /tmp this should drop back to ~8.4 ms/tuple. Linear extrapolation: 1M * 12.7 ms = 12.7k sec; 100k * 8.4 ms = 0.84k sec; ratio = 15.1x for 10x N (sublinear-to-linear borderline, attributable to contention not the genPolicy pipeline).
